claim: The top 1% of AI engineers build differently from the bottom 99%: reliable production systems require fundamentally different approaches than demos, focusing on reliability over looking good
actionable: When building AI agents for production, prioritize 80%+ reliability and bulletproof execution over 80% accuracy that only works in demos
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: Context engineering is the practice of optimizing model inputs and outputs to solve reliability problems; it bridges the gap between public open-source demos and enterprise production systems
actionable: Apply context engineering principles (careful input curation, output structuring, constraint satisfaction) when transitioning from prototype to production agent systems
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: As models improve (e.g., Opus 4.5 vs 4.0), engineers who invested time in context engineering can achieve similar results with prior-gen models, unlocking new capabilities with newer models
actionable: Invest in context engineering skills early; improved models will amplify your leverage if you've already mastered the craft
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: There will always be problems at the boundary of model capabilities; building reliable AI experiences means finding what the model struggles with and engineering context to solve it repeatedly
actionable: Identify the hardest task your agent must solve, then focus context engineering effort on making that specific task reliable before scaling
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: The 'dumb zone' is the 40% context window usage threshold; above 40%, diminishing returns occur for inexperienced users; experienced engineers can push to 60-70% if they know what they're doing
actionable: For learning, aim for 40% context window usage; as you gain intuition, experiment with pushing toward 60-70% for specific use cases
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: Building context engineering intuition requires reps (repeated practice); you must both overshoot (waste time over-engineering simple problems) and undershoot (fail on complex problems) to learn where to draw the line
actionable: Practice context engineering on diverse problems intentionally; track which problems you over-engineer and which you under-engineer to calibrate your judgment
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: Shipping reliable AI products differs from coding; coding means making software work locally, while shipping means deploying to production, fixing failures, and maintaining over time
actionable: When evaluating an agent system, measure success by production reliability and real user outcomes, not by coding speed or token throughput
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer

claim: High-leverage human roles in AI systems are those where humans solve problems AI cannot yet solve reliably; the role is to be selective, not replace the agent's work entirely
actionable: Design human-in-the-loop systems where humans intervene only on tasks the AI fails at, not on every output
source_title: Why Your AI Agents Don't Work with Dex Horthy of HumanLayer
